zone_6b_notes: Extremely long-lived perennial — peonies in Zone 6b Farmington can outlive the gardener who planted them (50–100 years in place). Plant bare-root divisions in fall (October) with eyes no more than 1–1.5 inches deep — too deep and they will not bloom. Blooms late April to May in stunning, fragrant double or single flowers. Missouri's winter chill satisfies dormancy requirement fully. Botrytis blight is common after rainy May weather — remove and dispose of infected stems. Ants on buds are harmless and not needed for blooming (old myth). No deadheading needed; fall frost kills foliage — cut to ground after killing frost.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant bare-root divisions in October — permanent location

water: moderate — 1 inch/week; drought tolerant once established
